name: 70s Pop Culture
description: Music, films, and cultural moments from the 1970s

questions.q: Which U.S. President resigned in August 1974 in the wake of the Watergate scandal?

source.notes: Resigned August 9, 1974. Succeeded by Gerald Ford, who pardoned him.

questions.q: Which country hosted the 1976 Summer Olympics?

source.notes: Held in Montreal. Known for Nadia Comăneci's perfect 10s.

questions.q: In which iconic 1974 boxing match did Muhammad Ali knock out George Foreman in Zaire?

source.notes: October 30, 1974. Ali used 'rope-a-dope' technique.

questions.q: Which legendary NYC disco nightclub opened in 1977 and became synonymous with celebrity excess?

source.notes: Opened April 26, 1977. Closed in 1980 after owners were arrested for tax evasion.

questions.q: NASA launched which probe in 1977 that eventually became the first human-made object to enter interstellar space?

source.notes: Launched September 5, 1977. Crossed into interstellar space in 2012.

questions.q: Which former Beatle released the song 'Imagine' in 1971?

source.notes: Title track of his second solo album. Co-written with Yoko Ono.

questions.q: Which band released the epic song 'Stairway to Heaven' on their untitled fourth album (Led Zeppelin IV) in 1971?

source.notes: Written by Jimmy Page and Robert Plant. Never released as a single.

questions.q: Which band released the operatic single 'Bohemian Rhapsody' in 1975?

source.notes: Written by Freddie Mercury. From the album 'A Night at the Opera.' Nearly 6 minutes long.

questions.q: Which group performed the disco hit 'Stayin' Alive' (1977), featured in 'Saturday Night Fever'?

source.notes: Barry, Robin, and Maurice Gibb. Tempo (100 BPM) used for CPR.

questions.q: Which Pink Floyd album, released in 1973, is famous for its prism cover and spent over 900 weeks on the Billboard 200?
choices: The Dark Side of the Moon

source.notes: One of best-selling albums ever (~45M copies). Cover designed by Hipgnosis.

questions.q: Which Eagles song with a 'dark desert highway' opening was released in 1977?

source.notes: Title track of their 5th studio album. Won Record of the Year Grammy (1978).

questions.q: Which Swedish pop group released 'Dancing Queen' in 1976?

source.notes: Their only US #1 hit. From the album 'Arrival.'

questions.q: Which 1971 Carole King album remains one of the best-selling albums in history, often featured in Greatest Albums lists?

source.notes: Over 25 million copies sold. Spent 15 weeks at #1 on Billboard 200.

questions.q: Which actor played Luke Skywalker in the original 'Star Wars' (1977)?

source.notes: Reprised role in sequel trilogy 2015-2019. Now also known for voice acting (The Joker).

questions.q: Which actor wrote and starred in the 1976 boxing film 'Rocky'?

source.notes: Wrote script in 3 days. Film won Best Picture over 'Network' and 'Taxi Driver.'

questions.q: Steven Spielberg's breakthrough 1975 summer blockbuster featured a terrorizing what?

source.notes: 'Jaws.' John Williams's score won an Oscar. Created the summer blockbuster season.

questions.q: In the 1973 Bond film 'Live and Let Die,' which actor debuted as James Bond?

source.notes: Moore played Bond in 7 films (1973-1985). Theme song by Paul McCartney & Wings.

questions.q: Who played Archie Bunker in the groundbreaking 1970s sitcom 'All in the Family'?

source.notes: O'Connor won 4 Emmys. Show ran 1971-1979 on CBS.

questions.q: Which actor starred as Hawkeye Pierce in the TV series 'M*A*S*H'?

source.notes: Ran 1972-1983. Series finale was the most-watched TV episode in US history.

questions.q: Which TV miniseries based on Alex Haley's novel about African-American heritage aired in 1977?

source.notes: Starred LeVar Burton as young Kunta Kinte. Watched by ~130 million Americans.
